name: pyflakes
documentation:
# Pyflakes

A simple program which checks Python source files for errors.

Pyflakes analyzes programs and detects various errors.  It works by
parsing the source file, not importing it, so it is safe to use on
modules with side effects.  It's also much faster.

It is [available on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/pyflakes/)
and it supports all active versions of Python: 3.6+.

## Installation

It can be installed with:

```
$ pip install --upgrade pyflakes
```

Useful tips:

- Be sure to install it for a version of Python which is compatible
  with your codebase: `python#.# -m pip install pyflakes` (for example,
  `python3.10 -m pip install pyflakes`)
- You can also invoke Pyflakes with `python#.# -m pyflakes .` if you want
  to run it for a specific python version.
- If you require more options and more flexibility, you could give a
  look to [Flake8] too.

## Design Principles

Pyflakes makes a simple promise: it will never complain about style,
and it will try very, very hard to never emit false positives.

Pyflakes is also faster than [Pylint]. This is
largely because Pyflakes only examines the syntax tree of each file
individually. As a consequence, Pyflakes is more limited in the
types of things it can check.

If you like Pyflakes but also want stylistic checks, you want
[flake8], which combines
Pyflakes with style checks against
[PEP 8] and adds
per-project configuration ability.

## Mailing-list

Share your feedback and ideas: [subscribe to the mailing-list](https://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/code-quality)

## Contributing

Issues are tracked on [GitHub](https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/issues).

Patches may be submitted via a [GitHub pull request].
If you are comfortable doing so, please [rebase your changes]
so they may be applied to main with a fast-forward merge, and each commit is
a coherent unit of work with a well-written log message.  If you are not
comfortable with this rebase workflow, the project maintainers will be happy to
rebase your commits for you.

All changes should include tests and pass [flake8].

```{image} https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/workflows/Test/badge.svg
:alt: GitHub Actions build status
:target: https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/actions
```

## Changelog

Please see [NEWS.rst](https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/blob/main/NEWS.rst).

[flake8]: https://pypi.org/project/flake8/
[github pull request]: https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/pulls
[pep 8]: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/
[pylint]: https://pylint.pycqa.org/
[rebase your changes]: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Branching-Rebasing

functions.name: pyflakes.reporter._makeDefaultReporter
documentation: Make a reporter that can be used when no reporter is specified.

classes.name: pyflakes.reporter.Reporter

methods.name: pyflakes.reporter.Reporter.__init__
documentation:
Construct a L{Reporter}.

@param warningStream: A file-like object where warnings will be
    written to.  The stream's C{write} method must accept unicode.
    C{sys.stdout} is a good value.
@param errorStream: A file-like object where error output will be
    written to.  The stream's C{write} method must accept unicode.
    C{sys.stderr} is a good value.

methods.name: pyflakes.reporter.Reporter.unexpectedError
pyflakes.reporter.Reporter.syntaxError
pyflakes.reporter.Reporter.flake
documentation:
pyflakes found something wrong with the code.

@param: A L{pyflakes.messages.Message}.
Formats the results of pyflakes checks to users.

modules.name: pyflakes.api
documentation: API for the command-line I{pyflakes} tool.

functions.name: pyflakes.api.check
pyflakes.api.checkPath
pyflakes.api.isPythonFile
documentation: Return True if filename points to a Python file.

functions.name: pyflakes.api.iterSourceCode
documentation:
Iterate over all Python source files in C{paths}.

@param paths: A list of paths.  Directories will be recursed into and
    any .py files found will be yielded.  Any non-directories will be
    yielded as-is.

functions.name: pyflakes.api.checkRecursive
pyflakes.api._exitOnSignal
documentation:
Handles a signal with sys.exit.

Some of these signals (SIGPIPE, for example) don't exist or are invalid on
Windows. So, ignore errors that might arise.

functions.name: pyflakes.api._get_version
documentation: Retrieve and format package version along with python version & OS used

functions.name: pyflakes.api.main
documentation: Entry point for the script "pyflakes".

modules.name: pyflakes.test.test_dict
documentation: Tests for dict duplicate keys Pyflakes behavior.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_dict.Test.test_duplicate_key_float_and_int
documentation:
These do look like different values, but when it comes to their use as
keys, they compare as equal and so are actually duplicates.
The literal dict {1: 1, 1.0: 1} actually becomes {1.0: 1}.

documentation: Provide the class Message and its subclasses.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.FutureFeatureNotDefined
documentation: An undefined __future__ feature name was imported.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.UnusedVariable
documentation:
Indicates that a variable has been explicitly assigned to but not actually
used.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.UnusedAnnotation
documentation:
Indicates that a variable has been explicitly annotated to but not actually
used.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.ReturnOutsideFunction
documentation: Indicates a return statement outside of a function/method.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.YieldOutsideFunction
documentation: Indicates a yield or yield from statement outside of a function/method.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.ContinueOutsideLoop
documentation: Indicates a continue statement outside of a while or for loop.
For whatever reason, Python gives different error messages for these two. We
match the Python error message exactly.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.BreakOutsideLoop
documentation: Indicates a break statement outside of a while or for loop.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.DefaultExceptNotLast
documentation: Indicates an except: block as not the last exception handler.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.TwoStarredExpressions
documentation: Two or more starred expressions in an assignment (a, *b, *c = d).

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.TooManyExpressionsInStarredAssignment
documentation: Too many expressions in an assignment with star-unpacking

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.IfTuple
documentation: Conditional test is a non-empty tuple literal, which are always True.

classes.name: pyflakes.messages.AssertTuple
documentation: Assertion test is a non-empty tuple literal, which are always True.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionName
documentation:
Exception names can't be used after the except: block.

The exc variable is unused inside the exception handler.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_namesDeclaredInExceptBlocks
documentation:
Locals declared in except: blocks can be used after the block.

This shows the example in test_undefinedExceptionName is
different.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringLocalVariable
documentation:
Exception names obscure locals, can't be used after.

Last line will raise UnboundLocalError on Python 3 after exiting
the except: block. Note next two examples for false positives to
watch out for.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringLocalVariable2
documentation:
Exception names are unbound after the `except:` block.

Last line will raise UnboundLocalError.
The exc variable is unused inside the exception handler.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringLocalVariableFalsePositive1
documentation:
Exception names obscure locals, can't be used after. Unless.

Last line will never raise UnboundLocalError because it's only
entered if no exception was raised.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delExceptionInExcept
documentation: The exception name can be deleted in the except: block.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringLocalVariableFalsePositive2
documentation:
Exception names obscure locals, can't be used after. Unless.

Last line will never raise UnboundLocalError because `error` is
only falsy if the `except:` block has not been entered.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringGlobalVariable
documentation:
Exception names obscure globals, can't be used after.

Last line will raise UnboundLocalError because the existence of that
exception name creates a local scope placeholder for it, obscuring any
globals, etc.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringGlobalVariable2
documentation:
Exception names obscure globals, can't be used after.

Last line will raise NameError on Python 3 because the name is
locally unbound after the `except:` block, even if it's
nonlocal. We should issue an error in this case because code
only working correctly if an exception isn't raised, is invalid.
Unless it's explicitly silenced, see false positives below.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringGlobalVariableFalsePositive1
documentation:
Exception names obscure globals, can't be used after. Unless.

Last line will never raise NameError because it's only entered
if no exception was raised.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedExceptionNameObscuringGlobalVariableFalsePositive2
documentation:
Exception names obscure globals, can't be used after. Unless.

Last line will never raise NameError because `error` is only
falsy if the `except:` block has not been entered.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_builtinWindowsError
documentation:
C{WindowsError} is sometimes a builtin name, so no warning is emitted
for using it.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_moduleAnnotations
documentation:
Use of the C{__annotations__} in module scope should not emit
an undefined name warning when version is greater than or equal to 3.6.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicGlobalsFile
documentation:
Use of the C{__file__} magic global should not emit an undefined name
warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicGlobalsBuiltins
documentation:
Use of the C{__builtins__} magic global should not emit an undefined
name warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicGlobalsName
documentation:
Use of the C{__name__} magic global should not emit an undefined name
warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicGlobalsPath
documentation:
Use of the C{__path__} magic global should not emit an undefined name
warning, if you refer to it from a file called __init__.py.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicModuleInClassScope
documentation:
Use of the C{__module__} magic builtin should not emit an undefined
name warning if used in class scope.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_magicQualnameInClassScope
documentation:
Use of the C{__qualname__} magic builtin should not emit an undefined
name warning if used in class scope.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_globalImportStar
documentation: Can't find undefined names with import *.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedByGlobal
documentation:
"global" can make an otherwise undefined name in another function
defined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedByGlobalMultipleNames
documentation: "global" can accept multiple names.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_globalInGlobalScope
documentation: A global statement in the global scope is ignored.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_global_reset_name_only
documentation: A global statement does not prevent other names being undefined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_unused_global
documentation: An unused global statement does not define the name.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_del
documentation: Del deletes bindings.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delGlobal
documentation: Del a global binding from a function.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delUndefined
documentation: Del an undefined name.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delConditional
documentation: Ignores conditional bindings deletion.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delConditionalNested
documentation:
Ignored conditional bindings deletion even if they are nested in other
blocks.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delWhile
documentation:
Ignore bindings deletion if called inside the body of a while
statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delWhileTestUsage
documentation:
Ignore bindings deletion if called inside the body of a while
statement and name is used inside while's test part.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_delWhileNested
documentation:
Ignore bindings deletions if node is part of while's test, even when
del is in a nested block.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_globalFromNestedScope
documentation: Global names are available from nested scopes.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_laterRedefinedGlobalFromNestedScope
documentation:
Test that referencing a local name that shadows a global, before it is
defined, generates a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_laterRedefinedGlobalFromNestedScope2
documentation:
Test that referencing a local name in a nested scope that shadows a
global declared in an enclosing scope, before it is defined, generates
a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_intermediateClassScopeIgnored
documentation:
If a name defined in an enclosing scope is shadowed by a local variable
and the name is used locally before it is bound, an unbound local
warning is emitted, even if there is a class scope between the enclosing
scope and the local scope.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_doubleNestingReportsClosestName
documentation:
Test that referencing a local name in a nested scope that shadows a
variable declared in two different outer scopes before it is defined
in the innermost scope generates an UnboundLocal warning which
refers to the nearest shadowed name.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_laterRedefinedGlobalFromNestedScope3
documentation:
Test that referencing a local name in a nested scope that shadows a
global, before it is defined, generates a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_nestedClass
documentation: Nested classes can access enclosing scope.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_badNestedClass
documentation: Free variables in nested classes must bind at class creation.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedAsStarArgs
documentation: Star and double-star arg names are defined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedAsStarUnpack
documentation: Star names in unpack are defined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_usedAsStarUnpack
documentation: Star names in unpack are used if RHS is not a tuple/list literal.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_unusedAsStarUnpack
documentation: Star names in unpack are unused if RHS is a tuple/list literal.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_keywordOnlyArgs
documentation: Keyword-only arg names are defined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_keywordOnlyArgsUndefined
documentation: Typo in kwonly name.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_annotationUndefined
documentation: Undefined annotations.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedInGenExp
documentation:
Using the loop variable of a generator expression results in no
warnings.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedInGenExpNested
documentation:
The loop variables of generator expressions nested together are
not defined in the other generator.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedWithErrorHandler
documentation:
Some compatibility code checks explicitly for NameError.
It should not trigger warnings.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedInClass
documentation: Defined name for generator expressions and dict/set comprehension.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedInClassNested
documentation: Defined name for nested generator expressions in a class.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedInLoop
documentation: The loop variable is defined after the expression is computed.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedFromLambdaInDictionaryComprehension
documentation:
Defined name referenced from a lambda function within a dict/set
comprehension.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_definedFromLambdaInGenerator
documentation:
Defined name referenced from a lambda function within a generator
expression.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedFromLambdaInDictionaryComprehension
documentation:
Undefined name referenced from a lambda function within a dict/set
comprehension.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.Test.test_undefinedFromLambdaInComprehension
documentation:
Undefined name referenced from a lambda function within a generator
expression.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.NameTests

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_undefined_names.NameTests.test_impossibleContext
documentation:
A Name node with an unrecognized context results in a RuntimeError being
raised.
Tests for some extra cases of name handling.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestImportationObject.test_unusedImport_underscore
documentation:
The magic underscore var should be reported as unused when used as an
import alias.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_aliasedImportShadowModule
documentation: Imported aliases can shadow the source of the import.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedIf
documentation:
Test that importing a module twice within an if
block does raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedIfElse
documentation:
Test that importing a module twice in if
and else blocks does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedTry
documentation:
Test that importing a module twice in a try block
does raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedTryExcept
documentation:
Test that importing a module twice in a try
and except block does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedTryNested
documentation:
Test that importing a module twice using a nested
try/except and if blocks does not issue a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedInNestedFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a global name with a nested function definition
generates a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedInNestedFunctionTwice
documentation:
Test that shadowing a global name with a nested function definition
generates a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedButUsedLater
documentation:
Test that a global import which is redefined locally,
but used later in another scope does not generate a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedBySubclass
documentation:
If an imported name is redefined by a class statement which also uses
that name in the bases list, no warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_redefinedInClass
documentation:
Test that shadowing a global with a class attribute does not produce a
warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_importInClass
documentation: Test that import within class is a locally scoped attribute.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_shadowedByFor
documentation:
Test that shadowing a global name with a for loop variable generates a
warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_shadowedByForDeep
documentation:
Test that shadowing a global name with a for loop variable nested in a
tuple unpack generates a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_usedInGlobal
documentation:
A 'global' statement shadowing an unused import should not prevent it
from being reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_usedAndGlobal
documentation:
A 'global' statement shadowing a used import should not cause it to be
reported as unused.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_assignedToGlobal
documentation:
Binding an import to a declared global should not cause it to be
reported as unused.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_importStar
documentation: Use of import * at module level is reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_importStar_relative
documentation: Use of import * from a relative import is reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_localImportStar
documentation: import * is only allowed at module level.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_packageImport
documentation: If a dotted name is imported and used, no warning is reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_unusedPackageImport
documentation:
If a dotted name is imported and not used, an unused import warning is
reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_duplicateSubmoduleImport
documentation:
If a submodule of a package is imported twice, an unused import warning
and a redefined while unused warning are reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_differentSubmoduleImport
documentation:
If two different submodules of a package are imported, no duplicate
import warning is reported for the package.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_used_package_with_submodule_import
documentation: Usage of package marks submodule imports as used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_used_package_with_submodule_import_of_alias
documentation: Usage of package by alias marks submodule imports as used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_unused_package_with_submodule_import
documentation: When a package and its submodule are imported, only report once.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_importedInClass
documentation: Imports in class scope can be used through self.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_importUsedInMethodDefinition
documentation: Method named 'foo' with default args referring to module named 'foo'.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_futureImport
documentation: __future__ is special.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_futureImportFirst
documentation: __future__ imports must come before anything else.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_futureImportUsed
documentation: __future__ is special, but names are injected in the namespace.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_futureImportUndefined
documentation: Importing undefined names from __future__ fails.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.Test.test_futureImportStar
documentation: Importing '*' from __future__ fails.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_ignoredInFunction
documentation:
An C{__all__} definition does not suppress unused import warnings in a
function scope.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_ignoredInClass
documentation: An C{__all__} definition in a class does not suppress unused import warnings.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_warningSuppressed
documentation:
If a name is imported and unused but is named in C{__all__}, no warning
is reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_augmentedAssignment
documentation: The C{__all__} variable is defined incrementally.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_list_concatenation_assignment
documentation: The C{__all__} variable is defined through list concatenation.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_tuple_concatenation_assignment
documentation: The C{__all__} variable is defined through tuple concatenation.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_unboundExported
documentation: If C{__all__} includes a name which is not bound, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_importStarExported
documentation: Report undefined if import * is used

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_importStarNotExported
documentation: Report unused import when not needed to satisfy __all__.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_usedInGenExp
documentation: Using a global in a generator expression results in no warnings.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_redefinedByGenExp
documentation:
Re-using a global name as the loop variable for a generator
expression results in a redefinition warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_usedAsDecorator
documentation:
Using a global name in a decorator statement results in no warnings,
but using an undefined name in a decorator statement results in an
undefined name warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_imports.TestSpecialAll.test_usedAsClassDecorator
documentation:
Using an imported name as a class decorator results in no warnings,
but using an undefined name as a class decorator results in an
undefined name warning.
Tests for suppression of unused import warnings by C{__all__}.

modules.name: pyflakes.test.test_api
documentation: Tests for L{pyflakes.scripts.pyflakes}.

functions.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.withStderrTo
documentation: Call C{f} with C{sys.stderr} redirected to C{stderr}.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.Node
documentation: Mock an AST node.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.SysStreamCapturing
documentation:
Context manager capturing sys.stdin, sys.stdout and sys.stderr.

The file handles are replaced with a StringIO object.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.LoggingReporter

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.LoggingReporter.__init__
documentation:
Construct a C{LoggingReporter}.

@param log: A list to append log messages to.
Implementation of Reporter that just appends any error to a list.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_emptyDirectory
documentation: There are no Python files in an empty directory.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_singleFile
documentation:
If the directory contains one Python file, C{iterSourceCode} will find
it.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_onlyPythonSource
documentation: Files that are not Python source files are not included.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_recurses
documentation:
If the Python files are hidden deep down in child directories, we will
find them.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_shebang
documentation:
Find Python files that don't end with `.py`, but contain a Python
shebang.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_multipleDirectories
documentation:
L{iterSourceCode} can be given multiple directories.  It will recurse
into each of them.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestIterSourceCode.test_explicitFiles
documentation:
If one of the paths given to L{iterSourceCode} is not a directory but
a file, it will include that in its output.
Tests for L{iterSourceCode}.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_syntaxError
documentation:
C{syntaxError} reports that there was a syntax error in the source
file.  It reports to the error stream and includes the filename, line
number, error message, actual line of source and a caret pointing to
where the error is.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_syntaxErrorNoOffset
documentation:
C{syntaxError} doesn't include a caret pointing to the error if
C{offset} is passed as C{None}.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_syntaxErrorNoText
documentation:
C{syntaxError} doesn't include text or nonsensical offsets if C{text} is C{None}.

This typically happens when reporting syntax errors from stdin.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_multiLineSyntaxError
documentation:
If there's a multi-line syntax error, then we only report the last
line.  The offset is adjusted so that it is relative to the start of
the last line.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_unexpectedError
documentation: C{unexpectedError} reports an error processing a source file.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestReporter.test_flake
documentation:
C{flake} reports a code warning from Pyflakes.  It is exactly the
str() of a L{pyflakes.messages.Message}.
Tests for L{Reporter}.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.makeTempFile
documentation: Make a temporary file containing C{content} and return a path to it.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.assertHasErrors
pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.getErrors
pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_missingTrailingNewline
documentation:
Source which doesn't end with a newline shouldn't cause any
exception to be raised nor an error indicator to be returned by
L{check}.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_checkPathNonExisting
documentation: L{checkPath} handles non-existing files.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_multilineSyntaxError
documentation:
Source which includes a syntax error which results in the raised
L{SyntaxError.text} containing multiple lines of source are reported
with only the last line of that source.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_eofSyntaxError
documentation:
The error reported for source files which end prematurely causing a
syntax error reflects the cause for the syntax error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_eofSyntaxErrorWithTab
documentation:
The error reported for source files which end prematurely causing a
syntax error reflects the cause for the syntax error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_nonDefaultFollowsDefaultSyntaxError
documentation:
Source which has a non-default argument following a default argument
should include the line number of the syntax error.  However these
exceptions do not include an offset.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_nonKeywordAfterKeywordSyntaxError
documentation:
Source which has a non-keyword argument after a keyword argument should
include the line number of the syntax error.  However these exceptions
do not include an offset.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_invalidEscape
documentation: The invalid escape syntax raises ValueError in Python 2

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_permissionDenied
documentation:
If the source file is not readable, this is reported on standard
error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_pyflakesWarning
documentation:
If the source file has a pyflakes warning, this is reported as a
'flake'.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_encodedFileUTF8
documentation: If source file declares the correct encoding, no error is reported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_CRLFLineEndings
documentation: Source files with Windows CR LF line endings are parsed successfully.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_misencodedFileUTF8
documentation:
If a source file contains bytes which cannot be decoded, this is
reported on stderr.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_misencodedFileUTF16
documentation:
If a source file contains bytes which cannot be decoded, this is
reported on stderr.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_checkRecursive
documentation:
L{checkRecursive} descends into each directory, finding Python files
and reporting problems.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.CheckTests.test_stdinReportsErrors
documentation: L{check} reports syntax errors from stdin
Tests for L{check} and L{checkPath} which check a file for flakes.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.getPyflakesBinary
documentation: Return the path to the pyflakes binary.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.runPyflakes
pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.test_goodFile
documentation:
When a Python source file is all good, the return code is zero and no
messages are printed to either stdout or stderr.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.test_fileWithFlakes
documentation:
When a Python source file has warnings, the return code is non-zero
and the warnings are printed to stdout.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.test_errors_io
documentation:
When pyflakes finds errors with the files it's given, (if they don't
exist, say), then the return code is non-zero and the errors are
printed to stderr.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.test_errors_syntax
documentation:
When pyflakes finds errors with the files it's given, (if they don't
exist, say), then the return code is non-zero and the errors are
printed to stderr.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.IntegrationTests.test_readFromStdin
documentation: If no arguments are passed to C{pyflakes} then it reads from stdin.
Tests of the pyflakes script that actually spawn the script.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_api.TestMain
documentation: Tests of the pyflakes main function.

modules.name: pyflakes.test.test_other
documentation: Tests for various Pyflakes behavior.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedInGenerator
documentation:
Test that reusing a variable in a generator does not raise
a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedInSetComprehension
documentation:
Test that reusing a variable in a set comprehension does not raise
a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedInDictComprehension
documentation:
Test that reusing a variable in a dict comprehension does not raise
a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition with another one raises a
warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedUnderscoreFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition named with underscore doesn't
raise anything.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedUnderscoreImportation
documentation: Test that shadowing an underscore importation raises a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedClassFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition in a class suite with another
one raises a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedIfElseFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition twice in an if
and else block does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedIfFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition within an if block
raises a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedTryExceptFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition twice in try
and except block does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedTryFunction
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition within a try block
raises a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_redefinedIfElseInListComp
documentation:
Test that shadowing a variable in a list comprehension in
an if and else block does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_functionDecorator
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition with a decorated version of
that function does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classFunctionDecorator
documentation:
Test that shadowing a function definition in a class suite with a
decorated version of that function does not raise a warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_unaryPlus
documentation: Don't die on unary +.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_undefinedBaseClass
documentation:
If a name in the base list of a class definition is undefined, a
warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classNameUndefinedInClassBody
documentation:
If a class name is used in the body of that class's definition and
the name is not already defined, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classNameDefinedPreviously
documentation:
If a class name is used in the body of that class's definition and
the name was previously defined in some other way, no warning is
emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classRedefinition
documentation: If a class is defined twice in the same module, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_functionRedefinedAsClass
documentation: If a function is redefined as a class, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classRedefinedAsFunction
documentation: If a class is redefined as a function, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classWithReturn
documentation: If a return is used inside a class, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_moduleWithReturn
documentation: If a return is used at the module level, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classWithYield
documentation: If a yield is used inside a class, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_moduleWithYield
documentation: If a yield is used at the module level, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_classWithYieldFrom
documentation: If a yield from is used inside a class, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_moduleWithYieldFrom
documentation: If a yield from is used at the module level, a warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_defaultExceptLast
pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_starredAssignmentNoError
documentation: Python 3 extended iterable unpacking

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_starredAssignmentErrors
documentation:
SyntaxErrors (not encoded in the ast) surrounding Python 3 extended
iterable unpacking

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_doubleAssignment
documentation:
If a variable is re-assigned to without being used, no warning is
emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_doubleAssignmentConditionally
documentation:
If a variable is re-assigned within a conditional, no warning is
emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_doubleAssignmentWithUse
documentation:
If a variable is re-assigned to after being used, no warning is
emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_comparison
documentation:
If a defined name is used on either side of any of the six comparison
operators, no warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_identity
documentation:
If a defined name is used on either side of an identity test, no
warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_containment
documentation:
If a defined name is used on either side of a containment test, no
warning is emitted.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_loopControl
documentation: break and continue statements are supported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_ellipsis
documentation: Ellipsis in a slice is supported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_extendedSlice
documentation: Extended slices are supported.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_varAugmentedAssignment
documentation:
Augmented assignment of a variable is supported.
We don't care about var refs.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_attrAugmentedAssignment
documentation:
Augmented assignment of attributes is supported.
We don't care about attr refs.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_globalDeclaredInDifferentScope
documentation: A 'global' can be declared in one scope and reused in another.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.Test.test_function_arguments
documentation: Test to traverse ARG and ARGUMENT handler

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_unusedVariable
documentation:
Warn when a variable in a function is assigned a value that's never
used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_unusedUnderscoreVariable
documentation:
Don't warn when the magic "_" (underscore) variable is unused.
See issue #202.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_unusedVariableAsLocals
documentation: Using locals() it is perfectly valid to have unused variables

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_unusedVariableNoLocals
documentation: Using locals() in wrong scope should not matter

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_unusedReassignedVariable
documentation: Shadowing a used variable can still raise an UnusedVariable warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_variableUsedInLoop
documentation:
Shadowing a used variable cannot raise an UnusedVariable warning in the
context of a loop.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignToGlobal
documentation:
Assigning to a global and then not using that global is perfectly
acceptable. Do not mistake it for an unused local variable.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignToNonlocal
documentation:
Assigning to a nonlocal and then not using that binding is perfectly
acceptable. Do not mistake it for an unused local variable.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignToMember
documentation:
Assigning to a member of another object and then not using that member
variable is perfectly acceptable. Do not mistake it for an unused
local variable.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignInForLoop
documentation: Don't warn when a variable in a for loop is assigned to but not used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignInListComprehension
documentation:
Don't warn when a variable in a list comprehension is
assigned to but not used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_generatorExpression
documentation:
Don't warn when a variable in a generator expression is
assigned to but not used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assignmentInsideLoop
documentation: Don't warn when a variable assignment occurs lexically after its use.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_tupleUnpacking
documentation:
Don't warn when a variable included in tuple unpacking is unused. It's
very common for variables in a tuple unpacking assignment to be unused
in good Python code, so warning will only create false positives.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_listUnpacking
documentation: Don't warn when a variable included in list unpacking is unused.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_closedOver
documentation: Don't warn when the assignment is used in an inner function.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_doubleClosedOver
documentation:
Don't warn when the assignment is used in an inner function, even if
that inner function itself is in an inner function.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_tracebackhideSpecialVariable
documentation:
Do not warn about unused local variable __tracebackhide__, which is
a special variable for py.test.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_ifexp
documentation: Test C{foo if bar else baz} statements.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_if_tuple
documentation: Test C{if (foo,)} conditions.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementNoNames
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using inside or after a nameless C{with}
statement a name defined beforehand.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementSingleName
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using a name defined by a C{with} statement
within the suite or afterwards.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementAttributeName
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using an attribute as the target of a
C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementSubscript
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using a subscript as the target of a
C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementSubscriptUndefined
documentation:
An undefined name warning is emitted if the subscript used as the
target of a C{with} statement is not defined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementTupleNames
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using any of the tuple of names defined by
a C{with} statement within the suite or afterwards.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementListNames
documentation:
No warnings are emitted for using any of the list of names defined by a
C{with} statement within the suite or afterwards.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementComplicatedTarget
documentation:
If the target of a C{with} statement uses any or all of the valid forms
for that part of the grammar (See
U{http://docs.python.org/reference/compound_stmts.html#the-with-statement}),
the names involved are checked both for definedness and any bindings
created are respected in the suite of the statement and afterwards.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementSingleNameUndefined
documentation:
An undefined name warning is emitted if the name first defined by a
C{with} statement is used before the C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementTupleNamesUndefined
documentation:
An undefined name warning is emitted if a name first defined by the
tuple-unpacking form of the C{with} statement is used before the
C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementSingleNameRedefined
documentation:
A redefined name warning is emitted if a name bound by an import is
rebound by the name defined by a C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementTupleNamesRedefined
documentation:
A redefined name warning is emitted if a name bound by an import is
rebound by one of the names defined by the tuple-unpacking form of a
C{with} statement.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementUndefinedInside
documentation:
An undefined name warning is emitted if a name is used inside the
body of a C{with} statement without first being bound.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementNameDefinedInBody
documentation:
A name defined in the body of a C{with} statement can be used after
the body ends without warning.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_withStatementUndefinedInExpression
documentation:
An undefined name warning is emitted if a name in the I{test}
expression of a C{with} statement is undefined.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_dictComprehension
documentation: Dict comprehensions are properly handled.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_setComprehensionAndLiteral
documentation: Set comprehensions are properly handled.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_exceptWithoutNameInFunction
documentation:
Don't issue false warning when an unnamed exception is used.
Previously, there would be a false warning, but only when the
try..except was in a function

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_exceptWithoutNameInFunctionTuple
documentation:
Don't issue false warning when an unnamed exception is used.
This example catches a tuple of exception types.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_augmentedAssignmentImportedFunctionCall
documentation:
Consider a function that is called on the right part of an
augassign operation to be used.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assert_without_message
documentation: An assert without a message is not an error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assert_with_message
documentation: An assert with a message is not an error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assert_tuple
documentation: An assert of a non-empty tuple is always True.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assert_tuple_empty
documentation: An assert of an empty tuple is always False.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assert_static
documentation: An assert of a static value is not an error.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_yieldFromUndefined
documentation: Test C{yield from} statement

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_f_string
documentation: Test PEP 498 f-strings are treated as a usage.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assign_expr
documentation: Test PEP 572 assignment expressions are treated as usage / write.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assign_expr_generator_scope
documentation: Test assignment expressions in generator expressions.

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestUnusedAssignment.test_assign_expr_nested
documentation: Test assignment expressions in nested expressions.
Tests for warning about unused assignments.

classes.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestIncompatiblePrintOperator

methods.name: pyflakes.test.test_other.TestIncompatiblePrintOperator.test_print_function_assignment
documentation: A valid assignment, tested for catching false positives.
Tests for warning about invalid use of print function.

modules.name: pyflakes.checker
documentation:
Main module.

Implement the central Checker class.
Also, it models the Bindings and Scopes.

functions.name: pyflakes.checker.parse_percent_format
documentation:
Parses the string component of a `'...' % ...` format call

Copied from https://github.com/asottile/pyupgrade at v1.20.1

functions.name: pyflakes.checker.counter
documentation:
Simplest required implementation of collections.Counter. Required as 2.6
does not have Counter in collections.

functions.name: pyflakes.checker.iter_child_nodes
documentation:
Yield all direct child nodes of *node*, that is, all fields that
are nodes and all items of fields that are lists of nodes.

:param node:          AST node to be iterated upon
:param omit:          String or tuple of strings denoting the
                      attributes of the node to be omitted from
                      further parsing
:param _fields_order: Order of AST node fields

functions.name: pyflakes.checker._is_typing_helper
documentation:
Internal helper to determine whether or not something is a member of a
typing module. This is used as part of working out whether we are within a
type annotation context.

Note: you probably don't want to use this function directly. Instead see the
utils below which wrap it (`_is_typing` and `_is_any_typing_member`).

functions.name: pyflakes.checker._is_typing
documentation:
Determine whether `node` represents the member of a typing module specified
by `typing_attr`.

This is used as part of working out whether we are within a type annotation
context.

functions.name: pyflakes.checker._is_any_typing_member
documentation:
Determine whether `node` represents any member of a typing module.

This is used as part of working out whether we are within a type annotation
context.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker._FieldsOrder
documentation: Fix order of AST node fields.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Binding
documentation:
Represents the binding of a value to a name.

The checker uses this to keep track of which names have been bound and
which names have not. See L{Assignment} for a special type of binding that
is checked with stricter rules.

@ivar used: pair of (L{Scope}, node) indicating the scope and
            the node that this binding was last used.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Definition
documentation: A binding that defines a function or a class.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Builtin
documentation: A definition created for all Python builtins.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.UnhandledKeyType
documentation: A dictionary key of a type that we cannot or do not check for duplicates.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.VariableKey
documentation:
A dictionary key which is a variable.

@ivar item: The variable AST object.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Importation

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Importation._has_alias
documentation: Return whether importation needs an as clause.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Importation.source_statement
documentation: Generate a source statement equivalent to the import.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Importation.__str__
documentation: Return import full name with alias.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.SubmoduleImportation
documentation:
A binding created by a submodule import statement.

A submodule import is a special case where the root module is implicitly
imported, without an 'as' clause, and the submodule is also imported.
Python does not restrict which attributes of the root module may be used.

This class is only used when the submodule import is without an 'as' clause.

pyflakes handles this case by registering the root module name in the scope,
allowing any attribute of the root module to be accessed.

RedefinedWhileUnused is suppressed in `redefines` unless the submodule
name is also the same, to avoid false positives.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.ImportationFrom.__str__
documentation: Return import full name with alias.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.StarImportation
documentation: A binding created by a 'from x import *' statement.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.FutureImportation
documentation:
A binding created by a from `__future__` import statement.

`__future__` imports are implicitly used.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Argument
documentation: Represents binding a name as an argument.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Assignment
documentation:
Represents binding a name with an explicit assignment.

The checker will raise warnings for any Assignment that isn't used. Also,
the checker does not consider assignments in tuple/list unpacking to be
Assignments, rather it treats them as simple Bindings.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.NamedExprAssignment
documentation: Represents binding a name with an assignment expression.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Annotation

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Annotation.redefines
documentation: An Annotation doesn't define any name, so it cannot redefine one.

Represents binding a name to a type without an associated value.

As long as this name is not assigned a value in another binding, it is considered
undefined for most purposes. One notable exception is using the name as a type
annotation.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.ExportBinding
documentation:
A binding created by an C{__all__} assignment.  If the names in the list
can be determined statically, they will be treated as names for export and
additional checking applied to them.

The only recognized C{__all__} assignment via list/tuple concatenation is in the
following format:

    __all__ = ['a'] + ['b'] + ['c']

Names which are imported and not otherwise used but appear in the value of
C{__all__} will not have an unused import warning reported for them.

class_variables.name: pyflakes.checker.Scope.importStarred
documentation: set to True when import * is found

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.FunctionScope

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.FunctionScope.unused_assignments
documentation: Return a generator for the assignments which have not been used.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.FunctionScope.unused_annotations
documentation: Return a generator for the annotations which have not been used.

I represent a name scope for a function.

@ivar globals: Names declared 'global' in this function.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.ModuleScope
documentation: Scope for a module.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.DoctestScope
documentation: Scope for a doctest.

classes.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.deferFunction
documentation:
Schedule a function handler to be called just before completion.

This is used for handling function bodies, which must be deferred
because code later in the file might modify the global scope. When
`callable` is called, the scope at the time this is called will be
restored, however it will contain any new bindings added to it.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.checkDeadScopes
documentation:
Look at scopes which have been fully examined and report names in them
which were imported but unused.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.differentForks
documentation: True, if lnode and rnode are located on different forks of IF/TRY

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.addBinding
documentation:
Called when a binding is altered.

- `node` is the statement responsible for the change
- `value` is the new value, a Binding instance

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.isDocstring
documentation:
Determine if the given node is a docstring, as long as it is at the
correct place in the node tree.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.GLOBAL
documentation: Keep track of globals declarations.

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.NAME
documentation: Handle occurrence of Name (which can be a load/store/delete access.)

methods.name: pyflakes.checker.Checker.CLASSDEF
documentation:
Check names used in a class definition, including its decorators, base
classes, and the body of its definition.  Additionally, add its name to
the current scope.
I check the cleanliness and sanity of Python code.
